resources.string: Accepted formats: MP3, OGG, WAV, AAC, FLAC, M4A
Tip: you can browse Recent, Audio or Downloads in your phone\'s file picker.
Tap the tattva symbol to select a custom sound
🔇 Phone is on silent — you won\'t hear the sound
📳 Phone is on vibrate — you won\'t hear the sound
Select a date to calculate Tattvas
Use GPS or search to add locations
Are you sure you want to delete \"%1$s\"?
This will delete all saved locations except București. This action cannot be undone.
Type at least 2 characters to search
Try a different search term\nor use (+) button to add manually
Tap to request your current location

The horse is a symbol of primordial power, speed and duality (the two Ashwini Kumars, physicians of the gods). Just as a horse cannot stand still, Ashwini energy is about pure initiative. The horse head symbol suggests a mind that always moves forward, being the first nakshatra to "break the ice" of the zodiac.

What to do: This is the ideal moment for anything that requires an explosive start. Begin medical treatments (healing is rapid here), buy vehicles, start a start-up business, practice speed sports or go on short trips. Excellent for putting into action new ideas that need courage.

What NOT to do: Not a good day for activities requiring tact, infinite patience or finalization. If you end something now, you may do it too hastily. Avoid monotonous tasks that offer no quick intellectual stimulation.

It represents the gate between worlds – birth and death. The Yoni symbolizes creative potential that passes through pain to bring something new into the world (labor). It is ruled by Yama (the god of death), so it carries energy of radical transformation and discipline.

What to do: Activities that involve "burning" old stages. Ideal for general cleaning, cutting ties with the past, strict fasting, fertility treatments or creative projects requiring massive effort. A good day for agriculture (deep planting).

What NOT to do: Avoid leisure trips or gentle negotiations. The energy is too intense and can become overwhelming for activities requiring calm. Don't start projects that need a smooth and obstacle-free path.

The knife or flame represents the ability to separate the useful from the useless. Krittika "cuts" impurities to reach the essence. It is the energy of the critic, the chef and the warrior who purifies through fire.

What to do: Excellent for cutting literally and figuratively: surgical interventions, haircuts, but also critical business analyses. This is the time to speak uncomfortable truths, cook elaborate meals or work with sharp tools/fire.

What NOT to do: Not a day for diplomacy or reconciliation. Weddings under Krittika are avoided because the "cutting" energy can affect the couple's unity. Avoid activities requiring a relaxed and lenient attitude.

The chariot is a symbol of civilization, the transport of harvest and prosperity. Rohini is considered the Moon's favorite due to its beauty and fertility. This is when growth becomes stable and sensual.

What to do: Anything related to construction, beauty and luxury. One of the best days for marriage, moving furniture into a house, planting a garden or opening a savings account. Everything started under Rohini tends to grow and flourish spectacularly.

What NOT to do: Avoid demolitions or harsh criticism. Not a day to destroy old structures, but to nourish what already exists. Any action involving cruelty or deprivation of comfort will go poorly.

The deer is always searching for fresh grass, symbolizing the curious, agile yet slightly fearful mind. The logic of this nakshatra is exploration and gathering information.

What to do: Ideal for research, study, light socializing, exploratory travel and artistic activities. An excellent day for clothes shopping, starting a new course or moving to a new home, as it brings fresh energy.

What NOT to do: Don't make radical or fixed decisions. Avoid direct confrontations or activities requiring iron will, as deer energy is rather oscillating and avoids conflict.

The teardrop symbolizes both suffering and the emotional cleansing that follows a storm (Rudra, the storm god, governs here). It is about the necessary destruction for a new cycle to begin.

What to do: Activities involving the elimination of broken things: demolishing a building, breaking an addiction, confronting an enemy or difficult technical repairs. A good day for releasing repressed emotions through intensive therapy.

What NOT to do: Don't start important journeys, weddings or new businesses based on harmony. Any "optimistic" beginning under Ardra risks being hit by an unexpected crisis requiring a painful reassessment.

The arrows returning to their quiver after hitting their target (or missing it) symbolize the recovery of resources. It is the nakshatra of "those who return", of regeneration and kindness. The logic is safety and repeating success.

What to do: Excellent for repairs of any kind (relationships, objects), starting higher studies, medical recovery treatments and house construction. A lucky day for getting a second chance in a legal or professional matter.

What NOT to do: Don't lend money to others, as the "return" energy also applies to possessions, and you may not see the money back soon. Avoid actions requiring abrupt and definitive finality.

The cow is the supreme symbol of selfless nourishment in Vedic culture. Pushya offers protection and nutrition to any project. It is considered the most beneficial nakshatra for any constructive activity, being ruled by Jupiter.

What to do: Starting any business, investments, acts of charity, education, spiritual practices, prayers and celebrations. If you want something to succeed long-term and be "blessed", choose Pushya.

What NOT to do: Tradition says Pushya should not be used for marriages, as it is so pure and spiritual that its energy doesn't suit the sensual and contractual aspect of a wedding.

The serpent represents hidden wisdom, secret protection, but also venom. The logic is survival and guarding territory. It is a hypnotic, intense and sometimes manipulative energy.

What to do: Activities requiring absolute discretion, complex business strategies, eliminating parasites (literally or symbolically), kundalini yoga and esoteric studies. Good for securing your position against competitors.

What NOT to do: Avoid activities requiring total honesty and openness (for example, a first romantic date). Don't lend valuable objects and don't start partnerships based only on "given word", as suspicion dominates this day.

The throne represents authority inherited from ancestors (Pitris). The logic is the continuity of lineage and honor. Magha connects us with our roots and the pride of belonging to a solid structure.

What to do: Honoring parents, visiting birthplaces, official ceremonies, taking on leadership positions, donations to established institutions. An excellent day for seeking a mentor's advice or organizing a grand event.

What NOT to do: Don't plan the distant future with overly avant-garde ideas or experimental technologies. Magha rejects novelty for novelty's sake; it wants respect for tradition and established rules.

The front part of the bed represents the beginning of rest and pleasure. It is governed by Bhaga, the god of fortune and joy. The logic of this energy is recreation after effort, sensuality and magnetizing luck through relaxation.

What to do: Artistic activities, romantic dates, parties, luxury shopping, fashion design and diplomacy. The perfect moment to ask for a favor or resolve a conflict through personal charm.

What NOT to do: Heavy physical labor, starting a strict diet or any activity requiring an austere attitude. Not a good day for starting projects demanding cold logical rigor.

It represents the second part of rest, the one preparing us to rise from bed and assume social responsibilities. It symbolizes stability in relationships and loyalty to community.

What to do: Marriages, signing long-term contracts, inaugurating offices, charity and taking on leadership roles. One of the best days for laying solid foundations in a friendship or business.

What NOT to do: Abrupt ending of relationships or social isolation. Not a day for selfish activities or ignoring previously made promises.

The hand represents the ability to work, seize opportunities and create through craftsmanship. The logic is concrete manifestation: transforming an idea into something you can touch.

What to do: Crafts, painting, massage, sales (where you need to "convince" with hand on heart), sports requiring dexterity, magic/tricks and gardening. An excellent day for quick commercial negotiations.

What NOT to do: Abstract planning without action. Not a good day for rest or activities that take excessively long without a tangible result.

A jewel is not born sparkling — it is polished. It symbolizes perfection of form and divine architecture (Vishwakarma). The logic is visible aesthetics and the internal structure that sustains beauty.

What to do: Interior design, fashion, architecture, precision repairs (watchmaking, IT), cosmetic surgery and everything related to visual arts. The moment to "polish" a project until it becomes flawless.

What NOT to do: Routine, dirty or boring activities. Not a day for deep investigations into the "mud" of problems, but for staying on the sparkling surface.

The blade of grass bending under the wind (Vayu) survives the storm, while the rigid tree falls. The logic is adaptability and freedom of movement. It represents the wind of change and the independence of spirit.

What to do: Commercial activities, marketing, public events, travel, education and anything requiring diplomacy. Excellent for starting something that needs flexibility to grow.

What NOT to do: Activities requiring firmness, punishing someone or an authoritarian attitude. Grass cannot be "tough", so neither will you succeed through brute force today.

An arch decorated with flowers, yet still an arch (weapon). It represents focused ambition and victory that comes after titanic effort. The logic is "the end justifies the means" and perseverance to the finish.

What to do: Competitions, legal argumentation, setting unreachable objectives for others, sustained efforts for success and political activities. A day for "conquering" new territory.

What NOT to do: Long leisure trips or delicate diplomacy. The energy is too aggressively positive to be empathetic with others' needs.

The lotus blooms in murky waters, symbolizing purity of heart and the ability to maintain friendships in difficult conditions. The logic is devotion and cooperation.

What to do: Organizing groups, scientific research, meditation, loyalty, meetings with friends and nighttime activities. The perfect moment for strengthening soul bonds.

What NOT to do: Direct confrontations, legal proceedings or solitary activities where you feel isolated. Not a good day for being a "lone wolf".

The umbrella protects, and the earring symbolizes authority gained through age and experience. The logic is protective power and crisis management from a superior position.

What to do: Managing family or a company, taking on major responsibilities, protecting the vulnerable and solving urgent problems. A day to act as a "patriarch/matriarch".

What NOT to do: Not the moment to appear insecure or ask others for help. Avoid activities requiring exaggerated sensitivity; today you must be the pillar of support.

The roots are in darkness, but they sustain life. The logic is radical investigation: you must dig deep, even if it hurts, to find the truth or to uproot the evil.

What to do: Deep research, psychotherapy, investigations, cutting vices, gardening (root vegetables) and meditation. Excellent for understanding the hidden causes of a failure.

What NOT to do: Financial loans or starting a marriage. The "uprooting" energy can cause projects started now to lack long-term stability.

The winnowing basket separates wheat from chaff. It represents purification through selection and the power of water that cleanses. The logic is invincibility through clarity: knowing what deserves to be kept and what must be discarded.

What to do: Confronting enemies, overcoming obstacles through courage, water-related activities (swimming, hydrotherapy), improving public image and war strategies (or competitive business).

What NOT to do: Activities requiring tact and fine diplomacy. This is a day of "separation", so peace negotiations may fail by excluding one of the parties.

The elephant's tusk (Ganesha) symbolizes immense strength that removes obstacles, but with royal dignity. The logic is lasting success through moral integrity.

What to do: Signing vital contracts, beginnings that must last a lifetime, legal matters and taking oaths. One of the most stable nakshatras for any serious beginning.

What NOT to do: Abrupt completion of stages or immoral activities. Any action started now "under the table" will turn against you with the force of an elephant.

The ear represents oral tradition and the ability to learn through active listening. The logic is receiving correct information before acting. It is about wisdom that comes from silence.

What to do: Learning, counseling, courses, administrative organization, travel and activities requiring attention to nuances. An excellent day for listening to experts' advice.

What NOT to do: Taking major risks on your own or making impulsive decisions without consulting anyone. Don't ignore the warnings received today.

Musical instruments are hollow inside to produce sound. The logic is rhythm and harmony with the group. It represents wealth that comes through collaboration and synchronization with the "music" of the universe.

What to do: Music, dance, activities involving high technology, real estate acquisitions, teamwork and marketing. A day to be "in the spotlight".

What NOT to do: Activities requiring total silence, isolation or a solitary routine. Not a day to be withdrawn; success comes from interaction.

A circle protecting a hundred stars. It represents mystery and unconventional healing. The logic is that sometimes truth is hidden and requires a technological or esoteric approach to be revealed.

What to do: Alternative therapies, scientific research, IT, astrology, treating chronic illnesses and meditating on life's secrets. Good for putting a "fence" of protection around your privacy.

What NOT to do: Marriage or social interactions requiring total emotional openness. The energy is one of an "observer", slightly cold and detached.

It represents spiritual fire and the "deathbed" of the ego. The logic is transformation through sacrifice. It is an intense, often bizarre or radical energy that forces you to look inward.

What to do: Harsh spiritual discipline, giving up toxic habits, activities requiring insane courage and confronting your own shadows. A day of purification through mental fire.

What NOT to do: Leisure trips, cheerful beginnings or activities requiring a light-hearted mood. The atmosphere is too serious and transformative for trivialities.

The serpent of the depths (Ahirbudhnya) represents calm wisdom from beneath the ocean of consciousness. The logic is peace and final stability. It is the energy that sustains the world without agitation.

What to do: Deep meditation, building foundations (physical or metaphorical), lifetime promises, charity and activities requiring infinite patience. The moment to "build" something that will never crumble.

What NOT to do: Activities requiring speed, agitation or quick reactions. Not a day for haste; today everything must flow slowly and steadily.

The fish swims in the cosmic ocean, representing the soul's journey toward the source. The logic is gentle finalization and protection in transition. It is the last station, where everything resolves with love and compassion.

What to do: Long journeys, starting a relationship based on pure love, artistic activities, charity, caring for animals and completing old projects. A day of divine grace.

What NOT to do: Harsh, rigid activities, punishments or confrontations. Don't start a "war" on the last day of the lunar zodiac; this is the moment for forgiveness and bright new horizons.
